Defer-items:
  - I-1 [autoid-lemma/large]: Order-m higher-order influence function projection-risk + product-remainder theory (Robins et al. 2008/2016): localized 
  - I-2 [mathlib-lemma/large]: Series/sieve L2 approximation (L^{-s/d}) + least-squares prediction rate O(L^{-s/d}+sqrt(L/N)) for Holder functions/dens
  - I-3 [mathlib-lemma/medium]: Classical interior local-polynomial bias O(h^eta)/variance O((Nh)^{-1/2}) theorem (Fan-Gijbels; Tsybakov). Absent in Cau
  - I-4 [autoid-lemma/large]: Bonvini-Kennedy same-class HOIF upper-bound assembly attaining rho_n on the continuous-dose class, on top of I-1..I-3 pl
Deferred consumer: The same-class UPPER endpoint R_n(P_{alpha,beta,s}) <= C rho

PROOF-REVIEW LOOP ESCALATION [unclear]: [scaffold-mismatch] Root cause is shared by all four Hölder assumptions (ass:mu-treatment-holder, ass:pi-treatment-holder, ass:mu-covariate-holder, ass:pi-covariate-holder): the helper defs `HolderBall1D`/`HolderBallND` in Basic.lean (lines 77-92) bound `iteratedDeriv`/`iteratedFDeriv` without requiring differentiability, so Lean's junk value 0 satisfies them and, for order ≥ 1, discontinuous functions inhabit the 'Hölder ball'; `⌊order⌋₊` also zeroes the Hölder exponent at integer orders. The notes are faithful — only the Lean rendering is broader — so re-scaffold the two ball defs to add `ContDiffOn ℝ ⌊order⌋₊ f S` (making the derivative bounds non-vacuous) and to use ℓ = largest integer STRICTLY below `order` for the modulus exponent; ass:local-positivity is unaffected.

After ANY hand de-launder you MUST run F3.5→F5: disclose every added assumption in state.json added_assumptions, confirm laundering_count returned to 0, and make the bank call yourself. SKILL §'F2–F4 loop'.
